counterfactual.explanation: Deleting a non-bridge edge keeps the graph connected; deleting a bridge edge disconnects it. The bridge property (no alternative path between endpoints) is what determines whether connectivity is preserved under edge deletion.

Edge deletion can only increase or preserve component count; edge addition can only decrease or preserve it. The two operations are not symmetric, and a bridge under deletion has no analogue under addition (every cut vertex is created by deletion, not addition).
